content: We can frame Self-Refine’s iterative single-model self-improvement loop as a primitive base case that can be augmented by the multiple inconsistent perspectives central to LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that maintains Self-Refine’s key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data while boosting refinement quality beyond what single-perspective self-feedback can achieve. This framework would have the LLM generate multiple divergent initial outputs for a given task (from different sampling temperatures or prompt framing) rather than one, then have each output critique the others to collect cross-perspective inconsistent feedback before each iteration of self-refinement.
